name: yap
description: Syslog daemon for BlueyOS — Let me tell you about my day!

scripts.preinst:
#!/bin/bash
# Pre-installation script for yap
# Called before files are placed.
set -e

# Create the syslog user/group if they do not exist
if ! getent group syslog >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    groupadd -r syslog 2>/dev/null || true
fi

if ! getent passwd syslog >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    useradd -r -g syslog -s /sbin/nologin \
        -d /var/log -c "yap syslog daemon" syslog 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# Ensure log directory exists
mkdir -p /var/log
chmod 0755 /var/log

# Ensure /var/run exists for the PID file
mkdir -p /var/run

exit 0
scripts.postinst:
#!/bin/bash
# Post-installation script for yap
# Called after files are placed.
set -e

echo "yap installed — Let me tell you about my day!"
echo "Claw service units are in /etc/claw/services.d/."
echo ""
echo "  yap starts as part of claw-basic.target."
echo "  yap-rotate runs as a claw timer service to rotate /var/log/system.log."
echo ""
echo "  Configuration: /etc/yap.yml"
echo "  Log output:    /var/log/system.log"
echo ""

exit 0
scripts.prerm:
#!/bin/bash
# Pre-removal script for yap
# Called before files are removed.
set -e

# Stop yap if claw is running
if command -v clawctl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    clawctl stop yap-rotate 2>/dev/null || true
    clawctl stop yap        2>/dev/null || true
fi

exit 0
scripts.postrm:
#!/bin/bash
# Post-removal script for yap
# Called after files are removed.
set -e

echo "yap removed — That was a big day, wasn't it!"
exit 0
